search_brief: Find a coding agent that changes source files, runs a test command, reads the failure, and makes another code edit without a human choosing the next patch.
Find an open-source system where an AI developer handles a GitHub issue by planning, editing, running checks, and revising the pull request.
Find a repo or product doc for a coding workflow that keeps state across multiple attempts and uses previous failure logs to improve later fixes.
Find a practical autonomous coding tool that optimizes a measurable target such as tests passing, benchmark score, lint status, or eval accuracy.
Find a coding agent implementation that records lessons or memory from failed code changes and reuses them on future tasks.
Find one system focused on code repair where the agent executes the project tests and decides the next edit from the observed traceback.
Find a long-running code agent that can continue work after CI fails and update its plan or patch based on the CI result.
Find an autonomous software engineering workflow that is more than an IDE chat assistant and includes a closed edit-run-evaluate loop.
Find a coding research project where LLMs generate candidate programs, benchmark them, and iterate toward better implementations.
Find a concrete tool that lets an AI coding agent optimize one metric over repeated code changes.
Find an AI coding agent that produces a patch, verifies it with commands, and self-corrects before presenting the final result.
Find a GitHub project from a credible engineer or lab where coding agents use eval feedback to improve model or agent behavior.
Find an autonomous research agent that starts from a hypothesis, writes experiment code, runs the experiment, analyzes results, and updates the research plan.
Find a system that automates scientific paper-style research by combining literature search, experiment execution, result interpretation, and drafting.
Find a GitHub repository for an AI scientist workflow that actually executes experiments rather than only summarizing papers.
Find a research automation platform with primary docs showing a repeated idea, implementation, evaluation, and revision cycle.
Find an autonomous research project that can launch training or simulation jobs and choose follow-up experiments from measured outcomes.
Find a tool where an LLM research agent writes code, runs benchmarks, and turns successful runs into a report or manuscript.
Find a recent AI research workflow that keeps an experiment log and uses it to decide the next research step.
Find a reusable autonomous research system, excluding surveys, paper lists, and cookbook-only examples.
Find a credible engineering writeup about an agent that conducts end-to-end computational research with automated evaluation.
Find an open-source research assistant that profiles candidate methods, executes validations, and iterates from failures.
Find a scientific discovery agent that uses code execution or data analysis as part of its feedback loop.
Find a self-improving agent system whose docs show it changing prompts, tools, skills, or policies after evaluating its own performance.
Find a GitHub project where an agent creates reusable skills from past successes and failures and applies them on later tasks.
Find an implementation of agents that train or tune themselves from task traces rather than only storing chat history.
Find a product or repo where feedback from completed tasks is converted into future behavior changes for an AI agent.
Find a self-evolving agent framework with an explicit loop for generating tasks, evaluating outcomes, and updating the agent.
Find a system that claims self-improvement and has primary-source evidence of the mechanism, not just marketing language.
Find an agent platform that uses memory plus evaluation to improve performance across separate runs.
Find an open-source autonomous agent that modifies its own instructions or capabilities after measured task results.
Find a credible example of an LLM agent that maintains a learning loop over time instead of a one-shot reflection step.
Find a self-improving agent project that is not a survey, benchmark collection, or awesome list.
Find a framework where agents improve their tool-use strategy based on feedback from failed actions.
Find a recursive improvement agent that proposes changes to its own prompt or code, evaluates them, and keeps the better version.
Find a system where an AI agent runs an improve-evaluate-select cycle over its own behavior or artifacts.
Find a repo that implements iterative prompt mutation, scoring, and promotion for an autonomous agent.
Find an AI workflow that recursively generates candidate improvements to a model, agent, or toolchain and validates each candidate.
Find a concrete recursive self-improvement experiment with a measurable evaluator and an automatic selection rule.
Find an autonomous agent that can inspect a failed run, propose a change to itself, and test whether the change improved performance.
Find a project where agents build improved successor agents or skills from prior task traces.
Find a practical implementation of self-revision where the agent updates instructions, memory, or code and reruns the same benchmark.
Find a recursive improvement loop for LLM agents that uses held-out tasks or eval suites to prevent regressions.
Find a system that automates the propose-test-adopt cycle for agent behavior changes.
Find an example of agent self-modification that is backed by primary docs and not only a conceptual paper.
Find a system where agent outputs are automatically scored and the score is used to change the next prompt, policy, or code path.
Find an LLM evaluation workflow that closes the loop by turning eval failures into agent improvements.
Find a project that uses LLM-as-judge results inside an autonomous improvement loop rather than only a dashboard.
Find an agent evaluation platform that can trigger prompt or workflow updates from failed test cases.
Find a repo where traces, annotations, or eval results become concrete future agent changes.
Find a practical system that repeatedly tests agent behavior, diagnoses failures, and applies improvements automatically.
Find a tool that combines benchmark execution with an agent that repairs or tunes itself from benchmark feedback.
Find an evaluation-driven agent improvement example from W&B, OpenAI, Anthropic, GitHub, or a credible AI startup.
Find an implementation where regression tests guard agent updates and failed tests drive the next attempt.
Find a closed-loop eval system for agents where the evaluator is part of the runtime improvement cycle.
Find a standalone project that does more than report metrics and actually uses those metrics to improve an agent.
Find an agent memory system where stored experiences change future decisions, not just a vector database for retrieval.
Find a workflow that writes failure memories or task lessons and uses them on later autonomous attempts.
Find an open-source project where memory is connected to task execution, evaluation, and improvement.
Find primary docs for an agent that keeps persistent state across runs and updates that state from outcomes.
Find an agent system that stores successful tool-use patterns and reuses them to improve future tasks.
Find a memory-backed coding or research agent with evidence that memories affect subsequent planning.
Find a practical implementation of episodic or skill memory inside a self-improving agent loop.
Find a long-running agent memory mechanism that records observations, failures, and corrected strategies.
Find a credible source about agent memory used for iterative behavior improvement rather than knowledge lookup only.
Find a runnable agent memory project that is not a resource list, benchmark list, or survey paper.
Find an AI agent whose memory is updated by evaluator feedback and later consulted by the same agent.
Find a long-running agent workflow that persists context over hours or days and reacts to intermediate failures.
Find a durable agent orchestration system with checkpoints, retries, and feedback-driven plan revision.
Find an autonomous workflow where an agent monitors progress, updates state, and continues after tool or CI failures.
Find a product or repo for agents that can carry a task across many steps while preserving decisions and observations.
Find a long-running coding or research agent that uses traces or logs from earlier steps to improve later actions.
Find a system for asynchronous agents that keeps working until a task reaches a measurable completion condition.
Find a framework for persistent agents where memory and evaluation influence future task execution.
Find an implementation of an agent that schedules follow-up work, checks results, and revises the plan without manual intervention.
Find a long-horizon agent workflow that is a concrete runnable system, not only an architecture blog post.
Find an autonomous agent platform with explicit support for retries, state, and self-correction over extended runs.
Find an AI workflow that can resume after interruption and use prior attempt data to avoid repeating mistakes.
Find an agent that detects its own failed action, diagnoses the cause, and retries with a changed strategy.
Find a self-correction workflow where the agent compares output against tests or criteria before finalizing.
Find a project that combines reflection with external validation, such as tests, benchmarks, or evaluator feedback.
Find a coding or research agent that performs critique, correction, and rerun cycles with primary-source evidence.
Find an implementation where failed tool calls or invalid outputs are turned into corrected next actions.
Find a self-correcting LLM agent that updates memory or instructions after repeated mistakes.
Find a system that uses verifier feedback to revise agent plans rather than simply regenerate answers.
Find an autonomous workflow that records why a previous attempt failed and changes the next attempt accordingly.
Find a concrete self-correction loop for agents that includes measurable success criteria.
Find a runnable project where agents repair their own outputs through evaluator-guided iteration.
Find a self-correction system for long-running agents that goes beyond one-turn reflection.
Find a system where AI designs experiments, executes them, analyzes the results, and chooses the next experiment automatically.
Find an autonomous ML experimentation loop that edits code, launches training or benchmarks, and iterates from metrics.
Find a lab, simulation, or software experimentation agent that follows a design-build-test-learn cycle.
Find a project for automated hypothesis validation where an agent runs falsification or sequential testing experiments.
Find an agentic framework that optimizes experiments using measured outcomes instead of manual researcher choices.
Find a multi-agent scientific workflow for experimental design, data analysis, and iterative optimization.
Find an open-source autonomous experimentation platform for ML, simulations, materials, biology, or software systems.
Find a practical implementation where an LLM controls experiment execution and uses success metrics for the next step.
Find an autonomous benchmark-driven experimentation system that is not merely a dataset or paper summary.
Find a closed-loop experiment agent with primary docs showing execution, evaluation, and revision.
Find a recent system that automates the full cycle from experiment proposal to result-driven follow-up.
